config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters a lot, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary by default, because once the state or majority can override them too easily, abuse follows. In my view, a healthy society is built by protecting people from unfair collective pressure, not by asking individuals to sacrifice their rights whenever it is convenient. Society should set limits only when there is a clear and serious harm to others, not simply because the majority wants it.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, hak individu justru menjadi batas penting agar mayoritas atau negara tidak terlalu mudah menginjak kebebasan orang, dan sejarah di sini penuh contoh ketika kekuasaan kolektif disalahgunakan atas nama “kebaikan bersama.” Tentu saja ada keadaan darurat dan aturan publik yang sah, seperti mencegah bahaya nyata bagi orang lain, tetapi itu berbeda dari menjadikan hak pribadi sekadar nomor dua secara default. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling sering menekan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi kebebasan sambil tetap menjaga ketertiban.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically override the rights of the individual, because that is exactly how abuse gets normalized. From an Indonesian perspective, I understand the appeal of social harmony and collective duty, but I also know how easily “for the public good” can become a justification for silence, pressure, and unfair treatment. Society should protect rights first, then restrict them only when there is a clear, serious, and concrete harm to others.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu; menurut saya hak individu tidak boleh diposisikan di bawah kepentingan masyarakat secara default. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat terlalu banyak contoh ketika “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, dan justru hak individu itulah pagar paling penting terhadap mayoritas yang sewenang-wenang. Memang ada batas yang sah, seperti saat ada bahaya nyata bagi orang lain atau kebutuhan darurat publik, tetapi itu pengecualian yang ketat, bukan prinsip utama. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah menekan orang, melainkan yang mampu menjaga ketertiban tanpa mengorbankan kebebasan dasar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the claim that society should automatically come before the individual, because in practice that often becomes an excuse to pressure people who are already vulnerable. From an Indonesian perspective, I value gotong royong and social harmony, but I have also seen how “for the common good” can be used to silence dissent or excuse unfair treatment. Society matters, but it should earn its authority by protecting rights first and restricting them only when there is a clear, concrete harm to others. If we reverse that order, the majority can too easily decide whose dignity is worth sacrificing.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara default. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat hak individu sebagai pagar utama terhadap penyalahgunaan oleh mayoritas maupun negara, dan sejarah di sini menunjukkan betapa mudahnya alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan pelanggaran. Saya setuju bahwa ada keadaan darurat dan bahaya nyata yang bisa membenarkan pembatasan tertentu, tetapi itu harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan orang yang paling rentan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
